# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

This is a curated knowledge base and resource list. We support the current live version of the repository.

| Version | Supported          |
| ------- | ------------------ |
| Latest  | :white_check_mark: |

## Reporting a Vulnerability

We take the security of the resources and links shared in this "Awesome" list seriously. If you discover a security-related issue (e.g., a link pointing to malware, a mirror with an invalid checksum, or an insecure technical guide), please report it immediately.

### How to report:

1.  **Do not open a public issue.**
2.  Contact the maintainer directly: [@KhanTheDaleK1](https://github.com/KhanTheDaleK1).
3.  Alternatively, if you are a security researcher, you may use GitHub's [Private Vulnerability Reporting](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/security-advisories/guidelines-for-reporting-a-vulnerability/reporting-a-vulnerability-to-a-repository-maintainer) feature if enabled on this repository.

### What to include:

-   The specific URL or resource affected.
-   A description of the potential security risk (e.g., "This mirror now serves a malicious executable").
-   Any evidence or steps to reproduce (if safe to do so).

## Safe Browsing Guidelines

While we strive to curate high-quality and safe resources:
-   **Always verify checksums** (if provided in `docs/checksums.txt`) when downloading binaries or large PDFs.
-   Be cautious when visiting third-party mirrors or community-driven forums (e.g., Reddit, KVR, Scribd).
-   Report any link that triggers browser security warnings.

Thank you for helping keep the Omnichord community safe.
